added: Streaming: send Chrome-style Sec-CH-UA / Sec-CH-UA-Platform hints on requests tied to major streaming hosts (may reduce 403s on app APIs); Settings → About explains Widevine license 400 and links to Castlabs EVS for production VMP signing of shipped builds
fixes: Windows packaging: electron-builder afterPack runs Castlabs EVS sign-pkg on the unpacked app before NSIS and Portable targets bundle it, so the installer and Portable.exe get the same VMP-signed Nebula.exe as dist/win-unpacked (post-dist signing only touched unpacked). Set NEBULA_SKIP_EVS=1 to skip when castlabs-evs is unavailable; NEBULA_EVS_SIGN=1 fails the build if sign-pkg errors (CI).

Networking: set an explicit Chromium disk-cache cap, disable QUIC (avoids some TLS handshake resets / net_error -101), and one-time remove broken HTTP Code Cache folders under userData (fixes Invalid cache current size from disk_cache)

Windows: pass Chromium --no-sandbox on startup so sandboxed helpers (including Widevine CDM) can read Nebula.exe — avoids sandbox_win.cc(789) “Sandbox cannot access executable” after portable/zip installs or strict AV, which blocked DRM streaming

DRM streaming: relax synchronous media permission checks that denied empty/unknown mediaType (blocked EME-style checks); auto-allow ambiguous media requests on major streaming origins; disable OS sandbox on tab webviews so Widevine CDM can load in guests; add adblock @@ exceptions for Netflix/Spotify/Crunchyroll CDNs and bump filter cache to v7

added: DRM streaming: switch to Castlabs Electron for Content Security (Widevine CDM via components.whenReady) so sites like Netflix, Spotify, and Crunchyroll can play protected video/audio; stricter third-party cookie rules skip stripping for more streaming/CDN hosts
For strict production VMP (some Netflix policies), see Castlabs EVS in the castlabs/electron-releases wiki — dev ECS builds are already VMP-signed for development.

Windows packaged updates: choosing Installer can download the NSIS setup from GitHub, quit Nebula, and launch the installer with silent mode (/S) so the existing install is upgraded without opening the browser first (falls back to opening the release or installer URL if auto-update is not available)

Updates: on startup (once per session when a newer release exists) and from Check for updates / the banner, a system dialog offers Installer (upgrade in place), Portable (keep existing install), or Not now; GitHub release assets are matched for direct downloads when present

Updates: optional GitHub release check (configure nebula.updateRepo or repository URL), banner when a newer release exists with Download and Dismiss, View → Check for updates, and a startup silent check
Tab media indicators: speaker when audio is playing or tab is muted (toggle mute), camera/microphone when capture is active (buttons attempt to stop tracks)

Picture in picture: View → Picture in picture (Ctrl+Shift+P) or right‑click a video → Picture in picture — opens Chromium’s floating PiP window (move, resize; stays above the main window). Works across frames when possible.
Bookmarks bar: favicon per site (http/https) next to each chip, loaded like tab icons; hidden if the icon fails to load

fixes: Address bar: search suggestions close when focus leaves the omnibox (e.g. clicking the page), and delayed DuckDuckGo results no longer reopen the list after you have moved away
Keyboard focus: after OAuth or other guest popups close, Nebula refocuses the main window and active tab webview; dismissing the save-password or permission overlay also returns focus to the page
Save password prompt only appears on commit-style capture (submit, login click, Enter, blur, etc.), not on every keystroke in the password field

Sign in with Google and similar OAuth flows: allow a real popup window (same browsing partition as tabs) instead of forcing the authorize URL into a new tab, which broke window.opener / postMessage handshakes

added: Login capture runs as a session preload on the browsing partition so it also loads inside iframes (not only the top webview frame); duplicate webview preload removed
Conservative fetch hook: successful POST/PUT/PATCH to auth-like URLs may capture password/username from JSON or urlencoded bodies
Vault export: optional outline export without password strings (checkbox); JSON includes exportRedacted when applicable
Vault import: choose merge (skip duplicate origin+username) or replace entire vault, with a confirm dialog for the destructive path
improvements: Saved passwords hint suggests exporting a backup when you have several entries

fixes: Vault export and import now require the same Nebula account unlock as viewing or editing passwords (export no longer bypassed the lock)

added: Saved passwords: filter (all / saved logins / session only / with password), Session badge on rows, Export… and Import… (JSON merge by origin+username)
Login capture: longer delayed reads after submit/click, password field blur and debounced input, DOM watch for late-mounted fields, pagehide snapshot, autocomplete=username detection

fixes: Session vault remove: use Electron clearData + explicit cookie removal so sites actually log out (clearStorageData(origin) alone was unreliable)

improvements: Removing a session-placeholder vault row (cookie-based “remember this site”) now clears that origin’s cookies and site storage on this device and reloads open tabs for that site so you are signed out there. Normal password-only rows still only delete the vault entry.

added: Session-aware vault hints: when you are already signed in via cookies but have no saved entry for that HTTPS site, Nebula may offer a placeholder row (empty password + note). View → Add this site to vault (session login)… adds one manually. Passwords cannot be read back from cookies—edit the entry if you want your password stored.
Optional local Nebula account (master password): create in Settings → Privacy; unlock in Saved passwords to view, edit, or copy stored passwords; Lock or wait ~15 minutes to lock again
Saved passwords vault (local only): add, edit, remove, copy username/password — View → Saved passwords or Settings → Privacy
Offer to save passwords after you submit a classic login form on HTTP(S) sites (not all single-page apps)
Site permission prompts when using Ask (camera, microphone, location, notifications, screen capture)
App-wide defaults for camera, microphone, location, notifications, and screen capture (Ask / Allow / Block)
improvements: Password vault file encrypted with OS secure storage when the platform supports it
Login detection: shadow DOM, delayed reads for React/Vue, login buttons & Enter on password — still best-effort (iframes / pure fetch logins may not fire)

added: Startup prompt to restore your last tabs or start fresh
Session snapshots saved as you browse (restored when you choose)
About shows app version from package.json
Update log with categorized notes — edit renderer/changelog.json when you ship; from repo root run npm run changelog:date to stamp today's date on the entry matching package.json
